meta.phase-name: Clerk auth completion and portal onboarding

intent.item: Finish the Clerk-primary cutover without breaking the deployed customer or staff portal.
Make route ownership explicit so customer, ops, invite, and onboarding flows stop guessing where “dashboard” means.
Keep Payload users and app-owned onboarding state authoritative even when Clerk hosts auth UX.

goals.goal: Canonicalize the portal routes around `/customer/dashboard` and `/portal/ops/dashboard`, while preserving compatibility redirects from the old paths.
Lock public account creation down to invite and claim flows when Clerk is active.
Complete the Clerk-first staff invite and first-login path into a one-time welcome modal on the ops dashboard.
Retire the remaining Supabase-first customer auth entry points and fallbacks that no longer belong in the product direction.

requirements.item: Existing bookmarks, email links, and internal tests must keep working through redirects while the canonical paths change.
The route migration must preserve staff-vs-customer navigation behavior, admin preview, and impersonation logic.
Welcome-dismissed state belongs to Grime Time data, not Clerk metadata.
Public sign-up should not reopen broad self-serve account creation while invite and claim flows are being hardened.

tasks.task: Route migration: move the customer and ops dashboard pages to `/customer/dashboard` and `/portal/ops/dashboard`, add compatibility redirects, update sidebar/auth defaults, and refresh tests that assume the old paths.
Public sign-up lockdown: remove or narrow remaining self-serve sign-up affordances when Clerk is active, keeping claim and invite completion working.
Staff invite onboarding: route invited staff through Clerk-hosted auth, bind or create the local user and membership, and show a one-time welcome modal on first arrival at `/portal/ops/dashboard`.
Supabase retirement: remove stale fallback auth surfaces that are no longer part of the supported product flow, then verify Stripe and Payload user linkage remain intact.

definition-of-done.item: The portal uses explicit canonical dashboard routes and the old paths only redirect.
Clerk-primary mode no longer presents broad public account creation outside invite or claim flow.
Staff can accept an invite, authenticate, land on the ops dashboard, dismiss the welcome modal once, and continue without seeing it on every visit.
Focused tests cover route redirects, onboarding state, and auth-surface expectations.
